data.title: BlackRock, Invesco challenge hedge funds with managed futures ETFs - report
data.description:
Asset managers BlackRock (NYSE:BLK), Invesco (NYSE:IVZ), and Fidelity Investments have launched a wave of exchange-traded funds intended to bring to retail traders strategies once available only to financial elites.

The three firms recently filed to start so-called managed futures ETFs, which use derivatives to ride trends across a range of asset classes, Bloomberg reported on Friday. Their systematic models are designed to adapt to shifting market conditions and offset downturns in traditional portfolios.

While hedge funds known as commodity trading advisers (or CTAs) dominate the trend-chasing strategy, controlling $340B in assets, their growth has stagnated, Bloomberg said, citing BarclayHedge data.

Meanwhile, the ETFs based on the strategy, though tiny by asset size, are growing quickly. Bloomberg data estimates that the ETFs now manage $3.3B, almost double the amount from a year ago. Their growth is helped by lower fees and better performance in 2024, due, in part, to their simpler investment style.

Average return from CTA-style ETFs increased ~7.3% last year, compared with the SG CTA Index, which tracks 20 hedge funds, which gained only 2.4% in the same period, the article said.

Relevant tickers: Simplify Managed Futures ETF (CTA), iMGP DBi Managed Futures Strategy ETF (DBMF), KraneShares Mount Lucas Managed Futures Index Strategy ETF (KMLM), WisdomTree Managed Futures Strategy ETF (WTMF).

data.title: Crypto-linked assets wrapped in red for February as bitcoin, ethereum plunge
data.description:
A number of crypto-linked stocks and exchange-traded funds were headed for losses in February as the selloff in bitcoin (BTC-USD) and ethereum (ETH-USD) put the cryptocurrencies in line for their worst monthly performance since FTX imploded in 2022.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) entered into February above $102K and has since tumbled. It was facing a monthly loss of ~19%, and earlier Friday risked a drop of ~23% when it fell below $79K. Ethereum (ETH-USD) was looking at a 30% slide for February, with the price down 4% Friday, around $2,221.

Among the factors driving crypto prices down: “President Donald Trump's announcement of impending tariffs … has heightened market uncertainties, leading to a sell-off in risk-sensitive assets, including cryptocurrencies,” Naeem Aslam, chief investment officer at Zaye Capital Markets, said.

Also, investor confidence was “shaken” after hackers stole ~$1.5B in digital assets from crypto exchange Bybit’s ethereum (ETH-USD) cold wallet this month, Aslam said.

Here's how some of crypto-linked ETFs and stocks have moved over the past month, through early Friday.

  • Grayscale Bitcoin Mini Trust ETF (NYSEARCA:BTC): -18.7%
  • Bitwise Bitcoin ETF (NYSEARCA:BITB): -18%
  • iShares Ethereum Trust ETF (ETHA): -30.7%
  • MicroStrategy (MSTR): -26.9%
  • Bit Digital (BTBT): -18.5%
  • Bitdeer Technologies (BTDR): -29%
  • Coinbase (COIN): -24.3%
  • IREN Ltd. (IREN): -16%
  • Mara Holdings (MARA): -21.6%
  • Riot Platforms (RIOT): -19.9%
  • Robinhood (HOOD): +4%

Other bitcoin-backed ETFs are: (IBIT), (ARKB), (GBTC), (BTCO), and (HODL).

Among blockchain ETFs: (BITQ), (DAPP), (BLOK), and (IBLC).

data.title: Eletrobras rises after reaching deal with Brazil over voting power, nuclear plant
data.description:
Eletrobras (NYSE:EBR) +4.3% in Friday's trading after reaching an agreement with Brazil's government in a mediation process that could allow the state to appoint three members of the company's board while freeing the company from fresh investments in the Angra 3 nuclear power plant.

Brazil owns more than 40% of Eletrobras (NYSE:EBR) common shares, but no more than 10% of the stake can be used to exercise voting power over company decisions under the terms of the company's 2022 privatization, which has been criticized by Brazil President Lula da Silva and prompted him to ask the Supreme Court to grant proportional voting power to the government, and the court then pushed the parties into a mediation process.

Under the new agreement, the 10% voting power cap for shareholders will be maintained, but the government will be able to appoint three members of Eletrobras' (EBR) board, which will be expanded to 10 from nine currently.

Eletrobras (EBR) said the two sides would work on a conciliation term to be approved by the company's shareholders and validated by Brazil's Supreme Court as part of the settlement deal.

The company also said it will no longer be obliged to allocate money for Angra 3's completion, but will keep guaranteeing 6.1B reais (~$1.05B) in loans previously obtained for the project during 40 years of off-and-on construction.

Analysts at Itau BBA praised the deal's overall terms as very positive for Eletrobras (EBR) and said having three government-nominated board members is in line with expectations.

data.title: Voya Financial upgraded at Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Morgan Stanley upgraded Voya Financial (NYSE:VOYA) to Overweight from Equalweight on Friday as the company's turnaround plan should drive a rebound in 2025 and set up an attractive growth profile for 2026 and beyond.

Concerns about the longer-term profitability of its Health Solutions segment have been allayed by management's moves to improve performance through new pricing and improved risk selection, said analyst Bob Jian Huang. Stop-loss underwriting normalization could kick in as soon as 2026, he added. Management expects core business growth in Wealth Solutions and Investment Management, supported by steady margins of 35%-39% in Wealth and ~29% in Investment Management.

The provider of workplace benefits and savings products has several paths for improvement, Huang noted. "If executed well, integrations of its recent acquisitions of OneAmerica in Wealth Solutions and Benefitfocus in Health Solutions should yield meaningful earnings benefits over time," he wrote in a note to clients.

Also, investments, such as enhancing capabilities in leave management and disability administration within Health Solutions, and expanding third-party insurance asset management presence, "should yield positive net impacts beyond 2025," Huang said.

Overall, "headwinds, particularly in Health Solutions, should abate, results should stabilize, and both EPS and ROE should inflect higher in the coming years," he said.

Voya (NYSE:VOYA) stock rose 2.4% in Friday morning trading.

Morgan Stanley's Overweight rating on Voya clashes with the SA Quant rating of Sell and aligns with the average Wall Street rating of Buy.

data.title: Perrigo posts record surge on investor day
data.description:
Shares of Perrigo (NYSE:PRGO) jumped ~19% to record potentially the stock's best intraday gain ever as the consumer healthcare firm detailed its near-term and long-term financial targets at its just-concluded Virtual Investor Day.

The two-hour event followed Ireland-based PRGO's Q4 2024 financials on Thursday, which exceeded Street forecasts for earnings but fell short of revenue expectations amid a ~2% YoY drop in net sales due mainly to forex headwinds and discontinued product lines.

Ahead of the investor day on Friday, the company set its 2025 outlook, indicating 1%-3% YoY and 2.5% - 4.5% YoY growth for its all-in and organic net sales, respectively, compared to ~6% YoY and ~5% YoY declines in 2024.

Its outlook for 2025 adj. diluted earnings per share stood at $2.90–$3.10, implying 13%–21% YoY growth compared to flat growth last year.

Additionally, Perrigo (NYSE:PRGO) announced its long-term outlook, indicating 2.5%–4.5% and high-single to low-double-digit percentage CAGR in organic net sales and adjusted diluted EPS, respectively.

data.title: LeMaitre cut to perform by Oppenheimer on valuation
data.description:
Oppenheimer has downgraded LeMaitre Vascular (NASDAQ:LMAT) to perform from outperform as its price target of $93 has been met.

The investment bank added it was downgrading the stock and removing its price target "as we question the longer-term sustainability of consistent price hikes that have led to LMAT's premium valuation."

LeMaitre (NASDAQ:LMAT) released its Q4 earnings report late Thursday, which was in line with expectations, according to Oppenheimer.

data.title: Vaycaychella plans up to 100M stock buybacks
data.description:
• Technology company Vaycaychella (OTCPK:VAYK) has proposed an up to 100M share repurchase through private deals.
  • The company said it would cancel the stocks.
  • The program is subject to approval of the mid-March board meeting.

data.title: Bitcoin’s slump could be a sign of a popping “bro bubble” – BofA
data.description:
Bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) inability to stay above $97,000 lately was the first sign of a “bro bubble” popping, said Michael Hartnett, chief investment strategist at BofA.

Bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) volume weighted average price – the number of bitcoins bought times Bitcoin’s price over the total number of bitcoins bought – since the U.S. election was $97,600.

Post-election volume weighted average prices need to hold “to prevent nervous ‘nouveau bulls’ selling,” said Hartnett in a note.

The cryptocurrency has lost more than 25% since its all-time-high on Jan. 20 as markets respond to tariffs announcements.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) was also the worst performing foreign exchange or currency vs. the U.S. dollar year-to-date across assets return, down 9.9%, according to BofA.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is down 11.16% year-to-date, down 18.6% in the past month, and down about 2% on Friday, currently trading at $83,806.

Also, on Wednesday, all U.S. bitcoin spot-listed exchange-traded funds saw outflows of more than $754M, with $3.6B ETF outflows since Feb. 7.

Here are Bitcoin-backed ETFs: (IBIT), (ARKB), (GBTC), (BRRR), (BTCO), (HODL), (BTCW), (FBTC), (BITB), (EZBC)

data.title: Allegro Microsystems gains amid takeover speculation
data.description:
• Allegro Microsystems (NASDAQ:ALGM) gained 2.3% amid some takeover speculation.
  • There's speculation that a large Western European company approached Allegro (NASDAQ:ALGM) about a deal, according to traders, who cited a Betaville "uncooked" alert that was circulating among traders on Friday.
  • Allegro Micorosystems (ALGM) has a market cap of $4 billion. The company is set to present at Morgan Stanley’s Technology, Media & Telecom conference on Tuesday.
